title: Investment Risk Tolerance Quiz - What's Your Investor Profile? | CrunchMilk
metaDescription: Discover your investment risk tolerance with this free 8-question quiz. Find out if you're conservative, moderate, or aggressive and match your portfolio accordingly.
tagline: What kind of investor are you really?

questions.question: How many years until you'll need to access this investment money?

options.text: Less than 3 years
3-7 years
7-15 years
15+ years

questions.question: If your portfolio dropped 30% in a market crash, what would you do?

options.text: Sell everything immediately to stop the bleeding
Sell some holdings and move to safer investments
Hold steady and wait for recovery
Buy more while prices are low

questions.question: How much investment experience do you have?

options.text: None - I'm completely new to investing
Some experience with basic funds or savings
Several years investing in stocks, bonds, and funds
Experienced investor comfortable with multiple asset classes

questions.question: Which portfolio allocation appeals to you most?

options.text: 90% bonds, 10% stocks - stability is everything
60% bonds, 40% stocks - mostly stable with some growth
60% stocks, 40% bonds - growth-focused with a cushion
90% stocks, 10% bonds - maximum growth potential

questions.question: How would you feel about a year where your portfolio earned 0% while others gained 15%?

options.text: Fine - I'm happy not losing money
A bit frustrated but I'd stick with my approach
Uncomfortable - I'd consider adjusting my strategy
Very frustrated - I want to be in growth investments

questions.question: How stable and predictable is your current income?

options.text: Very unstable - freelance, seasonal, or uncertain
Somewhat variable with a base income
Stable salary with good job security
Very stable with multiple income sources

questions.question: If you invested $10,000, what range of outcomes would you accept after one year?

options.text: $9,500 to $10,500 - I can't stomach losses
$8,500 to $11,500 - small swings are OK
$7,000 to $13,000 - willing to risk more for more
$5,000 to $16,000 - I'm in it for maximum long-term gains

questions.question: How do you generally make financial decisions?

options.text: I avoid decisions and stick with what feels safe
I research but lean toward conservative options
I analyze options and make calculated decisions
I'm decisive and comfortable with uncertainty for potential rewards

scoreRanges.label: Conservative Investor
scoreRanges.analysis: You prefer stability and capital preservation. Focus on high-quality bonds, CDs, and dividend-paying stocks. A portfolio of 70-80% bonds and 20-30% stocks may suit you. Your priority is protecting what you have.

scoreRanges.label: Moderate Investor
scoreRanges.analysis: You want growth but need a safety net. A balanced portfolio of roughly 50% stocks and 50% bonds could work well. Consider target-date funds or balanced funds that automatically manage risk for you.

scoreRanges.label: Growth Investor
scoreRanges.analysis: You're comfortable with volatility for higher returns. A 70-80% stock, 20-30% bond allocation matches your profile. Make sure you have enough time horizon to ride out downturns and consider diversifying internationally.

scoreRanges.label: Aggressive Investor
scoreRanges.analysis: You embrace risk for maximum growth potential. A portfolio of 90%+ stocks including small-caps and international exposure suits your profile. Ensure your time horizon supports this aggression and that you truly won't panic in downturns.
